senses.definition: relating to a fold or structure that connects the Eustachian tube area with the palate
examples: The salpingopalatine fold is located in the nasopharynx near the palate.
Surgeons describe the salpingopalatine region when discussing the anatomy of the area around the Eustachian tube.

senses.definition: inflammation or infection involving the fallopian tube and the lining of the abdominal cavity
examples: Doctors suspected salpingoperitonitis because the patient had pelvic pain and signs of infection.
Salpingoperitonitis can be a serious complication that needs urgent treatment.

senses.definition: a surgical procedure to reposition and fix a fallopian tube that has become displaced or twisted
examples: She had salpingopexy to correct the position of her fallopian tube.
The specialist recommended salpingopexy after the tube was found to be displaced.

senses.definition: relating to the part of the pharynx near the Eustachian (auditory) tube
examples: The salpingopharyngeal fold lies in the nasopharynx near the opening of the auditory tube.
Imaging showed an abnormality in the salpingopharyngeal area.

senses.definition: a muscle that connects the area of the auditory (Eustachian) tube to the pharynx and helps with swallowing
examples: The salpingopharyngeus muscle assists swallowing by working with nearby muscles.
Anatomy diagrams label the salpingopharyngeus along the pharynx.

senses.definition: relating to a structure that involves the pterygoid region of the skull and the tube-like (salping-) area
examples: The report mentions a salpingopterygoid connection in the anatomy.
The surgeon used the salpingopterygoid landmark during the procedure.

senses.definition: a surgical repair of a fallopian tube
examples: Salpingorrhaphy may be used to repair damage to a fallopian tube.
After the injury, the doctor discussed possible salpingorrhaphy.

senses.definition: an instrument used to examine the fallopian tube
examples: A salpingoscope can help doctors view the inside of the fallopian tube.
The procedure used a salpingoscope to investigate a blockage.

senses.definition: a term used for a specific surgical/technical structure or procedure name involving the fallopian tube and surrounding tissues (exact use varies by source)
examples: The text lists salpingostaphyline among the named surgical terms.
A specialist clarified what salpingostaphyline referred to in this context.

senses.definition: a specialized medical/anatomical term (meaning is not clear enough to define reliably for general learners)
examples: The document includes salpingostenochoria as a category name.
More context is needed to interpret salpingostenochoria correctly.

senses.definition: a surgical operation that creates an opening (stoma) involving the fallopian tube
examples: The surgeon planned salpingostomatomy to create a controlled opening.
Salpingostomatomy is described in some surgical references for specific tube problems.

senses.definition: plural of salpingostomy, meaning surgical openings involving the fallopian tubes
examples: Her history includes salpingostomies performed years earlier.
The report mentions multiple salpingostomies.

senses.definition: a surgery that creates an opening (stoma) connected with a fallopian tube
examples: A salpingostomy may be recommended when the tube needs to be opened or redirected.
The doctor explained the goals of the salpingostomy procedure.

senses.definition: plural of salpingotomy, meaning surgical incisions involving the fallopian tube
examples: The surgeon used several salpingotomies during the operation.
Her record lists past salpingotomies.

senses.definition: a surgical incision in a fallopian tube
examples: Salpingotomy is used in certain cases involving problems in the fallopian tube.
The doctor recommended a salpingotomy after imaging results.

senses.definition: another term for the fallopian tube
examples: The salpinx can become inflamed during an infection.
Doctors refer to the salpinx when describing tube anatomy.

senses.definition: plural of salpinx, meaning fallopian tubes
examples: The scan showed changes in both salpinxes.
Her notes mention inflammation in the salpinxes.

senses.definition: resembling the shape or characteristics of a salp (a type of marine animal) (specialized scientific use)
examples: The specimen was described as salpoid in form.
Biologists used the term salpoid to compare its body shape.

senses.definition: plural of salp, meaning a type of small marine animal that filters water
examples: Salps can be found in large numbers in the open ocean.
Researchers counted salps during the plankton survey.

senses.definition: plural form used for short forms/abbreviations of words that begin with "sal" (the exact meaning depends on context)
examples: In this biology class, "sals" refers to a specific set of terms.
Without the full form, sals is ambiguous in this document.

senses.definition: a spicy sauce, usually made from chopped tomatoes, onions, and peppers
examples: I like salsa on my tacos.
Can you pass the salsa, please?

senses.definition: a Latin American dance and music style
examples: They learned salsa last year.
We went to a salsa night downtown.

senses.definition: to make something into salsa, or to add salsa to something (rare/mostly technical or creative use)
examples: She salsaed the tomatoes with onion and chili.
The cookbook suggests salsaed vegetables for topping.

senses.definition: the act of making or adding salsa (uncommon/creative form)
examples: He kept salsaing the bowl until it tasted right.
They were salsaing chips with fresh dip.

senses.definition: plural of salsa, meaning different types of spicy sauces or dips
examples: There are many salsas at the party.
Try the mango salsas if you like sweet flavors.

senses.definition: a rare spelling/form that can refer to a type of sauce/dip related to salsa (meaning is unclear without context)
examples: The menu lists salse as a topping.
I’m not sure if salse here means salsa or something else.
